---
title: "03generate-plots"
format: html
---

```{r set up}
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(scales)
library(here)
library(plotly)
library(shiny)

clean_data<-read_csv(here("data","clean_data.csv"),show_col_types = FALSE)
between_subj<-read_csv(here("data","between_subj.csv"),show_col_types = FALSE)
```

```{r}


response_plot_data <- clean_data %>%
  mutate(
    subj_response_clean = factor(
      subj_response,
      levels = c("allgreen", "mostlygreen", "evensplit", "mostlyred", "allred"),
      labels = c("All\nGreen", "Mostly\nGreen", "Mixed", "Mostly\nRed", "All\nRed")
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(subj_response_clean))

ui <- fluidPage( #determines page layout, fluidPage uses rows and columns to organize displays 
  titlePanel("Subjective Color Responses"),
  
  sidebarLayout(#creates a sidebar for the dropdown menu
    sidebarPanel(
      selectInput( #makes dropdown menu for selecting data to be shown
        inputId = "subject_select",
        label = "Choose Subject View:",
        choices = c("All Subjects (Aggregated)", unique(as.character(response_plot_data$participant)))
      )
    ),
    
    mainPanel(
      # Interactive plot output
      plotlyOutput("color_plot", height = "600px")
    )
  )
)

#
server <- function(input, output, session) {
  
  output$color_plot <- renderPlotly({
    
#ifelse statement that determines what to display depending on what is selected from the dropdown menu (input)
    if (input$subject_select == "All Subjects (Aggregated)") {
      
      # Aggregate across ALL participants
      plot_data <- response_plot_data %>%
        count(subj_response_clean) %>%
        mutate(prop = n / sum(n))
      
      # Build the base plot without facets for showing just aggregate data
      p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = subj_response_clean, y = prop, fill = subj_response_clean)) +
        geom_col(width = 0.65)
      
    } else {
      
      # Filter for just the single selected participant
      plot_data <- response_plot_data %>%
        filter(participant == input$subject_select) %>%
        count(participant, subj_response_clean) %>%
        mutate(prop = n / sum(n))
      
      # Build the plot with a facet for that participant
      p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = subj_response_clean, y = prop, fill = subj_response_clean)) +
        geom_col(width = 0.65) +
        facet_wrap(~ participant)
    }
### apply customization    
    p <- p +
      scale_x_discrete(expand = expansion(mult = 0.15)) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = c( #from ggplot, allows to map color according values
        "All\nGreen" = "#1b7837",
        "Mostly\nGreen" = "#7fbf7b",
        "Mixed" = "#d9d9d9",
        "Mostly\nRed" = "#ef8a62",
        "All\nRed" = "#b2182b"
      )) +
      scale_y_continuous( #adjusts scale of y axis
        labels = percent_format(),
        limits = c(0, 1),
        expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))
      ) +
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "gray50") + #add horizontal line
      labs(
        title = "Subjective Color Responses",
        x = "Subjective Color Response",
        y = "Proportion of Trials"
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
      theme(
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 13, angle = 15, hjust = 1, lineheight = 0.9),
        panel.spacing = unit(1.2, "lines"),
        legend.position = "none"
      )
    
    ggplotly(p) #make graph interactive using plotly pkg
  })
}


shinyApp(ui = ui, server = server) # calls the Shiny app

```

```{r subj resp histogram}
response_plot_data <- all_data %>%
  mutate(
    subj_response_clean = factor(
      subj_response,
      levels = c("allgreen", "mostlygreen", "evensplit", "mostlyred", "allred"),
      labels = c("All\nGreen", "Mostly\nGreen", "Mixed", "Mostly\nRed", "All\nRed")
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(subj_response_clean)) %>%
  count(participant, subj_response_clean) %>%
  group_by(participant) %>%
  mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(response_plot_data, aes(x = subj_response_clean, y = prop, fill = subj_response_clean)) +
  
  geom_col(width = 0.65) +
  
  facet_wrap(~ participant, nrow = 2) +
  
  scale_x_discrete(
    expand = expansion(mult = 0.15)
  ) +
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "All\nGreen" = "#1b7837",
    "Mostly\nGreen" = "#7fbf7b",
    "Mixed" = "#d9d9d9",
    "Mostly\nRed" = "#ef8a62",
    "All\nRed" = "#b2182b"
  )) +
  
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent_format(),
    limits = c(0, 1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))
  ) +
  
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "gray50") +
  
  labs(
    title = "Individual Differences in Subjective Color Responses",
    x = "Subjective Color Response",
    y = "Proportion of Trials"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 13, angle = 15, hjust = 1, lineheight = 0.9),
    panel.spacing = unit(1.2, "lines"),
    legend.position = "none"
  )
```

```{r probe accuracy plot}
plot_data <- clean_data %>%
  mutate(
    trial_type_clean = case_when(
      trial_type == "control" ~ "Control",
      trial_type == "congruent" ~ "Probe = Imagined Color",
      trial_type == "incongruent" ~ "Probe \u2260 Imagined Color",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(trial_type_clean)) %>%
  group_by(participant, trial_type_clean) %>%
  summarise(
    prop_correct = mean(correct, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    trial_type_clean = factor(
      trial_type_clean,
      levels = c(
        "Control",
        "Probe = Imagined Color",
        "Probe \u2260 Imagined Color"
      )
    )
  )

summary_data <- plot_data %>%
  group_by(trial_type_clean) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_acc = mean(prop_correct, na.rm = TRUE),
    se = sd(prop_correct, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

accuracy_plot <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = trial_type_clean, y = prop_correct)) +
  
  geom_line(aes(group = participant),
            color = "gray75",
            alpha = 0.3,
            linewidth = 0.7) +
  
  geom_point(aes(group = participant),
             color = "gray70",
             alpha = 0.45,
             size = 2) +
  
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5,
             linetype = "dashed",
             color = "gray45",
             linewidth = 0.8) +
  
  geom_line(data = summary_data,
            aes(y = mean_acc, group = 1),
            color = "black",
            linewidth = 2) +
  
  geom_errorbar(data = summary_data,
                aes(
                  y = mean_acc,
                  ymin = mean_acc - se,
                  ymax = mean_acc + se
                ),
                width = 0.12,
                linewidth = 1) +
  
  geom_point(data = summary_data,
             aes(y = mean_acc),
             size = 4,
             color = "black") +
  
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 1),
    breaks = c(0, 0.5, 1),
    labels = c("0%", "50%", "100%")
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Accuracy by Trial Type",
    subtitle = "Accuracy is near ceiling across trial types",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Percent Correct"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 18) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14, angle = 18, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 15),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 24),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 15)
  )

ggsave(here("results","accuracy_plot.png"),accuracy_plot)
```
